edge_hypothesis: ema_rsi_atr has the highest ranging PF of any template (2.864) thanks to the ATR volatility gate filtering dead-money periods. The current ACCEPT uses RSI 40-70 (long) and 30-60 (short). Hypothesis: widening the RSI acceptance band (35-65 for both sides) captures more volatility-expansion entries that the narrower band misses, while the ATR gate still filters noise. A tighter band (45-55) tests the opposite: ultra-selective entries with higher average quality.
target_regime: ranging (ema_rsi_atr ranging PF=2.864 — highest of any template) + transitional (PF=1.731)

variants.name: ema_rsi_atr_widband_8to1
variants.description: EMA-RSI-ATR with wide RSI band (35-65) at 8:1 R:R. Captures more trade opportunities during volatility expansion while ATR gate maintains signal quality.

components.notes: EMA_9 vs EMA_21 for trend direction
RSI_14 in wide band (35-65) for entry confirmation
ATR_14 above 20-bar MA as volatility expansion gate

variants.name: ema_rsi_atr_widband_10to1
variants.description: EMA-RSI-ATR with wide RSI band (35-65) at 10:1 R:R. Same entry logic as widband_8to1 but wider TP to capture larger volatility-expansion moves. Tests whether extra TP room improves PF like it did for Supertrend and MACD families.

components.notes: EMA_9 vs EMA_21 for trend direction
RSI_14 in wide band (35-65) for entry confirmation
ATR_14 above 20-bar MA as volatility expansion gate

variants.name: ema_rsi_atr_tightband_8to1
variants.description: EMA-RSI-ATR with ultra-tight RSI band (45-55) at 8:1 R:R with wider 2.0 ATR stop. Only enters when RSI is near equilibrium during volatility expansion — filters out momentum-chasing entries. Fewer but higher-conviction trades. Wider stop gives breathing room.

components.notes: EMA_9 vs EMA_21 for trend direction
RSI_14 in tight band (45-55) for ultra-selective entry
ATR_14 above 20-bar MA as volatility expansion gate
